research_question: Which chess patterns unlock the most understanding per unit of time? (Item #2 from grandmaster_60min report's Further Research Needs)
mode: deep-research-pipeline (SCOPE -> PLAN -> RETRIEVE -> TRIANGULATE -> SYNTHESIZE -> CRITIQUE -> REFINE -> PACKAGE)
assumptions: 60-minute time compression is the constraint (board only, no technology)
CHREST model time parameters (~8-10s per chunk, ~60s per template) are accurate for estimating installation times
Pattern frequency from puzzle databases generalizes to real game frequency
The 'typical learner' is an adult novice (0-1200 Elo) with basic rules knowledge
Individual differences in learning style may affect the optimal sequence
Disco Chess platform data (431,899 puzzle attempts) is representative of the broader chess-improving population
The Zone of Proximal Development framework applies to chess pattern learning
Pattern synergy/interference relationships from CHREST simulations transfer to human learning
search_dimensions: Core tactical patterns ranked by frequency + leverage
Geodesic curriculum / optimal sequencing in skill space
CHREST model installation times per pattern type
Disco Chess & Woodpecker analytics on per-theme improvement rates
Chess pedagogy literature on what to teach first, second, third
Transitional problems and curriculum learning research
User-reported data (Reddit, Chess.com forums, Lichess community)
Platform analytics (Disco Chess, Lichess puzzle database, Chess.com stats)
key_findings: Fork, pin, skewer, and discovered attack (the 'Big Four') deliver ~5-10x the understanding-per-minute of advanced patterns
Removal of defender has highest multiplier effect (appears in 40-60% of multi-move combinations) despite being only 6th most common
The geodesic curriculum requires 4 foundation patterns -> 3 force multipliers -> 2 capstone patterns -> 1 cycling review session, fitting exactly in 60 minutes
Pattern plasticity varies dramatically: capturing defender improves +14.7pp via woodpecker vs pin +1.0pp
User-reported data strongly converges with academic findings on pattern priority
